concrete_goal: Add a Pomodoro timer for students to manage focus and break sessions with start, pause, reset, and completed-session tracking.
success_criteria: Students can start, pause, and reset focus or break sessions.
The timer displays the active mode, remaining time, and completed session count.
The timer preserves readable controls across desktop and mobile layouts.
scope_in: Pomodoro timer UI with focus and break modes.
Local timer state for active mode, remaining time, paused state, reset state, and completed sessions.
scope_out: User accounts, notifications, calendar integrations, analytics, paid APIs, and production deployment.
assumptions: Local browser state is sufficient for this timer.
Default focus and break durations can be used without external configuration.
implementation_constraints: Do not add external services or paid APIs.
Keep timer controls directly reachable from the timer view.

scenario_flows.name: Run Pomodoro session
preconditions: The Pomodoro timer is open with default focus and break durations.
steps: Reset the timer and complete a focus session.
scenario_flows.expected_result: Remaining time, active mode, pause state, reset state, and completed-session count update correctly.
failure_or_edge_cases: Invalid duration input shows validation and blocks saving.
A small mobile viewport keeps start, pause, reset, and mode controls readable.

verification_matrix.test: Run available tests or add a focused timer behavior test if the project has a test harness.
verification_matrix.behavior_check: Check start, pause, reset, validation, completed-session count, desktop layout, mobile layout, and keyboard focus behavior.

acceptance_evidence_plan: Desktop and mobile screenshots plus behavior, accessibility, and state coverage for the Pomodoro timer controls.
iteration_policy: Rework until the review score reaches the quality threshold.

delegation_plan_seed.objective: Add the Pomodoro timer product surface.
